seoTitle: How to Upload Receipts in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Digitize and upload receipts in QuickBooks Online to simplify expense tracking and reduce paperwork for your business.
intro: Uploading receipts digitally into QuickBooks Online cuts down on lost paperwork and makes expense tracking far more efficient, whether you're a freelancer or running a growing business. This guide covers uploading, organizing, and categorizing receipts so your records stay clean.

steps.heading: Access the receipt upload feature
steps.body: Navigate to the receipts section of QuickBooks Online where you can add new files.

steps.heading: Upload your receipt
steps.body: Add a photo or scanned image of the receipt directly into QuickBooks, either from your device or a connected mobile app.

steps.heading: Match the receipt to a transaction
steps.body: Link the uploaded receipt to the corresponding bank transaction or expense entry for accurate record-keeping.

steps.heading: Categorize the expense
steps.body: Assign the correct expense category to the receipt so it's properly reflected in your financial reports.

steps.heading: Review uploaded receipts periodically
steps.body: Check back regularly to make sure receipts are matched and categorized correctly, especially before tax season.

faq.question: Can I upload receipts from my phone directly into QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online supports mobile receipt capture, letting you photograph and upload receipts on the go.

faq.question: Does QuickBooks automatically match receipts to bank transactions?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online offers automated matching features that can connect uploaded receipts to related bank transactions, though manual review is still recommended.

faq.question: What file types can I upload as receipts?
faq.answer: Common image and document formats are typically supported for receipt uploads, making it easy to use scanned files or phone photos.

faq.question: Why should I digitize my receipts instead of keeping paper copies?
faq.answer: Digitizing receipts reduces the risk of losing paperwork, speeds up expense tracking, and makes it easier to stay organized for tax time.
